ai-zone.bots_page.block_all.title: Block AI Crawlers
ai-zone.bots_page.block_all.description:
Block AI Crawlers from scraping your websites and training LLMs without your consent.
Note: Blocking AI Bots will also block verified AI bots. <0>Learn more.
ai-zone.bots_page.block_all.toast: We have a newer version of this setting available. Make sure to enable it to help you block AI Crawlers.
ai-zone.bots_page.block_all.tooltip: This crawler is being blocked by the Block AI Bots security setting. <0>Disable it to control it in AI Crawl Control.
ai-zone.bots_page.preset_block.tooltip: This crawler is being blocked by the AI bot controls security setting. <0>Edit it to control it in AI Crawl Control.

ai-zone.description: Analyze and control how AI Crawlers access your content.
ai-zone.metrics_page.upsell.title: AI Crawler Traffic Analytics
ai-zone.metrics_page.upsell.description: Get metrics on how AI Crawlers interact with your site.
ai-zone.metrics_page.requests_chart.desc: Review AI crawler requests over time for your domain.
ai-zone.metrics_page.data_transfer_chart: Review data transferred by AI crawlers over time for your domain.
ai-zone.metrics_page.status_code_chart.d: View the distribution of HTTP status codes for AI crawler requests over time.
ai-zone.metrics_page.operator_summary.de: Analyze traffic referred by AI operators—track where visitors come from and which content they visit.
ai-zone.metrics_page.referrals_over_time: Track AI-driven traffic referrals to your site over time.
No referral data available for the selected time period
ai-zone.metrics_page.table.description.p: View the most crawled paths by AI crawlers.
Analyze path patterns to identify which sections of your site are most crawled.
ai-zone.metrics_page.stats.allowed_reque: Requests that received a successful response.
ai-zone.metrics_page.stats.unsuccessful_: Requests that did not receive a successful response due to a block, other security rules, or website errors.
ai-zone.metrics_page.stats.referrals.too: Number of visits originated from platforms owned by crawler operators.
ai-zone.robots_page.page_description: Monitor how AI crawlers interact with your robots.txt files across your hostnames. Track request patterns, verify file availability, and identify crawlers that violate your directives.
ai-zone.robots_page.availability.title: Availability
ai-zone.robots_page.availability.descrip: Monitor the request frequency and health status of robots.txt files.
ai-zone.robots_page.availability.table.t: The specific robots.txt file being requested.
Successful requests have 2xx status codes. Unsuccessful requests have 4xx or 5xx status codes.
The current status when accessing the live robots.txt file.
Indicates whether the robots.txt file contains Content Signals directives for usage in AI training, search, or AI input.
ai-zone.robots_page.toggle.title: Cloudflare managed
ai-zone.robots_page.toggle.description: When enabled, Cloudflare creates or updates your robots.txt file to signal that your content should not be used for AI training.
ai-zone.robots_page.toggle.tooltip: Cloudflare will manage your robots.txt file to signal that your content should not be used for AI training.
ai-zone.robots_page.agent_readiness.desc: Check if your site is ready for AI agents by testing your robots.txt and related configurations.
ai-zone.robots_page.violations.title: Violations
ai-zone.robots_page.violations.descripti: Identify AI crawlers requesting paths currently disallowed by your robots.txt. This list only shows violations on highest-traffic paths. Filter by Crawler, Operator, or Hostname to view detailed activity.
ai-zone.robots_page.violations.table.too: The bot that requested a path disallowed by robots.txt directives.
The specific URL (hostname and path) the crawler accessed.
The line from your robots.txt file that disallowed access to the path.
The total number of HTTP requests made to this specific URL.
ai-zone.robots_page.violations.empty_sta: No violations found with the current filters.
ai-zone.settings_page.stripe.action_requ: Additional information is required in Stripe
ai-zone.settings_page.stripe.disconnecte: Cloudflare partners with Stripe to help you receive payments and keep personal bank and details secure.
ai-zone.settings_page.stripe.modal.title: Connect to Stripe
ai-zone.settings_page.stripe.modal.descr: Connect to Stripe to receive payments by setting up or linking your account. You'll be redirected back to Cloudflare after connecting to Stripe.
Connect to your Stripe account to pay website owners.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.desc: Manage your website's pay per crawl settings.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.pric: Apply a flat rate to all requests from crawlers specifically marked to "Charge".
Require payment for all bot-scored traffic at the path level. Define specific exceptions for known crawlers.

Pay Per Crawl price (per request)
Crawlers scanning your website will be charged this price each time they make a request.
Set a rate to start charging
Price must be 0 or between %{minPrice} and %{maxPrice} %{code}
Price must be 0 or between %{minPrice} and %{maxPrice} %{code}
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.lice: Sets the URL to display in the Link header for responses to crawlers.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.defa: Whether new crawler additions should be charged or allowed.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.in_b: Allows the origin to include a <0>crawler-price</0> header in the responses.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.enab: Charging price will be set at $%{price} per request.
Charging price will be set by Pay Per Crawl worker rules.
Googlebot and crawlers already set to Block will not change.
You can modify individual crawler settings anytime in the Crawlers tab.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.disa: Are you sure you want to disable Pay Per Crawl? This will stop charging any crawlers.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.enab: Enable Pay Per Crawl for this zone?
Pay Per Crawl is currently disabled for this zone. Enabling it makes the feature available so you can activate it, configure pricing and start charging crawlers.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.upda: Failed to update Pay Per Crawl. Please try again.
ai-zone.settings_page.pay_per_crawl.zone: Pay Per Crawl is not enabled for this zone. <0>Enable</0> if you want to use it.
Pay Per Crawl is not enabled for this zone. Contact your account administrator to enable it.
ai-zone.settings_page.custom_response.de: Configure the response that blocked crawlers will receive when they attempt to access your website.
ai-zone.settings_page.custom_response.re: Customizable text to display to blocked crawlers.
If a message is provided, it will override the custom response set in <0>custom rules</0>.
An HTTP status code that describes the reason for the block.
ai-zone.settings_page.custom_response.er: Response message is required for this configuration
Response message content is too large. (2KB maximum)
ai-zone.settings_page.custom_response.pa: Paths excluded from blocking. Crawlers can still access these even when blocked.
ai-zone.settings_page.custom_response.al: After setting this crawler to Allow, any custom response message that has been set will be deleted. If you currently use a custom response message, you will need to re-enter this message under Configure response on the Security tab if you block another crawler in the future.
ai-zone.settings_page.zones.title: Domain Access
ai-zone.settings_page.zones.description: Select which domains should have access to Pay Per Crawl
ai-zone.settings_page.zones.search.label: Search
ai-zone.settings_page.zones.tooltips.sta: Indicates whether pricing is configured and crawlers can be set to Charge in AI Crawl Control.
ai-zone.settings_page.zones.tooltips.vis: Controls whether Pay Per Crawl is available in AI Crawl Control settings for the domain. Setting to Disabled will turn off Pay Per Crawl and stop charging for requests.

ai-zone.rules_engine.title: Pay Per Crawl pricing
ai-zone.rules_engine.description: Create custom price set to control AI crawler access based on request properties, bot scores, and more. Price sets are evaluated in order and the first matching price determines the action.
ai-zone.rules_engine.rules_list_descript: Manage your Pay Per Crawl price sets. Click on a price set to edit it or create a new price set.
ai-zone.rules_engine.learn_more: Learn more about Pay Per Crawl prices
ai-zone.rules_engine.dialog.title: Manage Pay Per Crawl pricing rules
ai-zone.rules_engine.dialog.subtitle: Configure pricing rules for AI crawler access
ai-zone.rules_engine.summary_card.title: Pay Per Crawl pricing
ai-zone.rules_engine.summary_card.worker: Worker is active with %{smart_count} rule |||| Worker is active with %{smart_count} rules
ai-zone.rules_engine.deployment.card_des: Create a Cloudflare Worker to set pricing for specific content. Define a price per path or content type, and select which bots can access for free.
ai-zone.rules_engine.deployment.worker_n: Spaces will be converted to dashes. Only alphanumeric characters, dashes, and underscores are allowed.
ai-zone.rules_engine.deployment.errors.w: Worker name must contain at least one alphanumeric character
ai-zone.rules_engine.deployment.errors.d: There was an error while deploying:

ai-zone.rules_engine.rule_builder.then_s: Charge a fee when the condition is met
ai-zone.rules_engine.rule_builder.unless: Skip charging if any of these exceptions apply

ai-zone.rules_engine.rules_list.empty_st: No price sets yet. Create your first price set to get started.

ai-zone.rules_engine.validation.rule_mis: Price set must have a bot score exception for WAF protection
ai-zone.rules_engine.action.title: Action
ai-zone.rules_engine.action.description: Choose what action to take when a request matches this rule
ai-zone.rules_engine.action.challenge_in: Challenge actions will present a verification challenge to the requester. Legitimate crawlers may not be able to complete challenges.
ai-zone.rules_engine.action.log_warning: Log action will only record the match without taking any action. Use this for testing rules before enforcement.
ai-zone.rules_engine.custom_response.inf: Configure a custom response message and status code for blocked requests
ai-zone.rules_engine.custom_response.con: Enter the message to display when a request is blocked. Maximum 10,000 characters.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.no_worker_: A WAF rule exists but the Pay Per Crawl worker was not found. The WAF rule may need to be removed.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.no_waf_but: The Pay Per Crawl worker exists but no WAF rule was found. Create rules below to enable bot blocking.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.no_worker_: The Pay Per Crawl worker has no routes configured for this zone. <0>Add a route</0> to enable the worker.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.config_has: The worker has been modified and may harm site behavior. <0>Redeploy</0> to fix.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.config_par: Unable to read the Pay Per Crawl worker configuration. The config.json file may be corrupted or invalid.
ai-zone.rules_engine.warnings.config_waf: The WAF rule does not match the Worker configuration. <0>Redeploy</0> to fix.
ai-zone.rules_engine.delete_pricing.desc: This will permanently remove the Pay Per Crawl pricing worker and WAF rule. This action cannot be undone.
ai-zone.rules_engine.delete_pricing.succ: Pay Per Crawl pricing deleted successfully
ai-zone.rules_engine.delete_pricing.erro: Failed to delete Pay Per Crawl pricing
ai-zone.rules_engine.cleanup_resources.d: There are unused resources from a previous configuration that can be cleaned up.

ai-zone.terms_and_conditions.description: Site owners are compensated for their high-quality content, and AI crawlers get the valuable data they need to build the best AI experiences.
